description_long: This is a small wooden pagoda structure with a single, gently sloping roof featuring upward-curving eaves, characteristic of traditional Japanese architecture. The roof is covered with textured tiles, and the building sits on a circular patch of green, grassy terrain with stone paving at the entrance. The front facade displays two lanterns hanging under the roof, and a small open shelter with a stone well or basin is attached to one side. The walls are constructed from horizontal wooden planks, and the roof is supported by sturdy wooden beams. The structure appears to be a shrine or small ceremonial building, with a simple yet elegant design and natural materials.
description: A small wooden Japanese-style pagoda with a tiled, upward-curving roof, lanterns, and a side shelter over a stone well.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, lanterns, and the attached side shelter with a stone well.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back wall and the full expanse of the tiled roof, with the side shelter visible.
description_view_2: Opposite front view focusing on the solid wooden back wall and the roof's decorative curves.
description_view_3: Direct front view with both lanterns, the stone-paved entrance, and the side shelter clearly visible.
